purpose: Operationalize scheduler signal isolation with diagnostics visibility, runbook command paths, and calibration reporting over recent publisher history.
design_intent: Make opportunity-signal adaptation observable and repeatable for operators without changing publisher fail-closed behavior or control-panel authority semantics.
change_impact: Affects diagnostics API payloads, overview dashboards, replay report outputs, and operator runbook procedures for scheduler tuning.

in: Expose isolated scheduler signal summary in diagnostics APIs
Add operator-facing replay runbook command path with timestamped output
Emit calibration-oriented replay artifact for multiplier bound tuning
Advance P4 plan/task tracker status after validation
out: Changing publisher success URL criteria or fail-closed submission semantics
Introducing new scheduler control knobs in the API

trend_insights.fallback: null when publisher history diagnostics cannot be built

calibration_policy.series: isolatedMultiplier over adaptation windows (or global fallback when no windows)

recommendation_logic.tighten_bounds: isolatedBoundHitRate == 0 and suggested bounds fall strictly inside current bounds

acceptance_criteria: Trend insights payload includes schedulerSignals diagnostics without breaking existing callers
Dedicated scheduler signal diagnostics endpoint returns contract-shaped payload
Runbook command writes timestamped replay artifacts and calibration report
P4 plan/task trackers are advanced to done after lint and integration verification
